name: Hans Hedemann Surf School
tagline: Founded by a former Pipeline pro — the gold standard for Waikiki surf lessons with guaranteed stand-ups and zero bro energy.
price: $$ — Group lesson ~$80, semi-private ~$135, private ~$200 (2 hrs, board included)
parking: Park at Waikiki Shell lot or Kapiolani Park; school meets at the T&C Surf shop in the Park Shore Waikiki hotel
hours: Daily 8 AM–4 PM; lessons every 2 hours
bestTime: 8 AM first session for smallest crowds and cleanest waves; book 2–3 days ahead in peak season
kamaaina: Kamaaina rates available — ask when booking
talkStory: Hans Hedemann grew up on Oahu and competed at Pipeline in the '80s and '90s before founding his surf school in 2001. His instructors are all certified watermen — not random backpackers — and the school has maintained the highest safety record in Waikiki for over two decades. They teach at Canoes and Queens breaks using soft-top longboards and a patient, methodical approach. The 4:1 student-to-instructor ratio means you actually get coached, not just pushed into whitewater and abandoned. They also run an advanced program for intermediates wanting to progress to shortboards. Located inside the Park Shore Waikiki hotel lobby, which means real changing rooms and showers — a luxury most surf schools skip.
